personas.name: The Parisian Minimalist
personas.summary: Architect, editor, or senior creative who treats Celine as her professional uniform. The brand's rigorous simplicity mirrors her own aesthetic sensibility — she buys Celine because nothing else is edited enough.
personas.shopping_behavior: Deliberate and unhurried. Buys fewer pieces but wears them constantly. Drawn to silhouette and proportion over surface detail. Asks about fabric weight and drape. Dislikes being sold to.
personas.trend_receptivity: Structural silhouette updates, precise tailoring narratives, material quality stories, clean seasonal colour shifts
personas.avoid: Anything with embellishment, print-heavy pieces, overtly trend-forward styling, decorative hardware

personas.name: The Power Dresser
personas.summary: Senior executive or founder who uses Celine to dress with authority without effort. The brand lets her look considered and serious without thinking too hard about fashion — it does the work for her.
personas.shopping_behavior: Efficiency-driven buyer who values a CA who understands her life. Builds a working wardrobe, not a fashion collection. Responds to occasion-based framing and outfit-building conversations.
personas.trend_receptivity: Versatile investment pieces, workwear-appropriate silhouettes, wardrobe-anchoring items, quality and longevity narratives
personas.avoid: Overly fashion-forward pieces, anything requiring significant styling effort, seasonal novelties that won't carry over

personas.name: The Intellectual Newcomer
personas.summary: Fashion-literate younger client discovering Celine as the grown-up evolution of her taste. She's read about Phoebe Philo and follows Hedi Slimane's creative output — she has strong opinions and wants a peer-level conversation.
personas.shopping_behavior: Highly researched before arriving. Knows the archive and current collection. Often starts with leather goods. Wants the CA to engage with her knowledge, not explain basics.
personas.trend_receptivity: Creative direction references, runway-to-retail pieces, bags with strong design history, pieces with editorial context
personas.avoid: Generic luxury framing, talking down to her knowledge level, pushing obvious or entry-level pieces

personas.name: The Quiet Achiever
personas.summary: High-net-worth client who has consciously moved away from visible status symbols. Celine's logoless authority is the point — she wants to be recognised as someone with taste, not someone with a logo.
personas.shopping_behavior: Long consideration cycle before purchasing. Values in-store consultation and a trusted SA relationship. Asks about craftsmanship and care. Rarely influenced by social media.
personas.trend_receptivity: Material and craft narratives, logoless statement pieces, subtle seasonal updates to core silhouettes, investment framing
personas.avoid: Any overt branding or logo moments, trend-driven pieces, anything that signals effort over ease
